payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the latest active memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because it has been explicitly superseded by mem_new, and superseded memories are not shown as current recall results. This preserves the current state while avoiding presentation of outdated or replaced facts.
payload.decision: Recall preferred the newer Goldman-sourced memory for Sam and excluded the older one because the older record was marked as superseded by a later conflicting update about the same subject.

entity_resolution.reason: Both memories refer to the same subject, Sam, so recall resolves them to one entity and keeps only the non-superseded version.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new was returned because it is the active memory for Sam from Goldman after conflict resolution.
mem_old was filtered because it is superseded and should not be presented as current recall.

details: Resolved Sam as the shared subject across both memories.
Identified mem_new as current and mem_old as superseded.
Applied recall policy to exclude superseded memories from current results.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has a superseded/inactive status flag.
Verify that mem_new is linked to mem_old via a supersedes relation.
If both should remain visible, change retrieval policy to include historical memories separately from current recall.
If supersession was applied incorrectly, remove the supersedes link and reindex both memories.

raw_text: {"intent":"explain_recall_result","decision":"Recall preferred the newer Goldman-sourced memory for Sam and excluded the older one because the older record was marked as superseded by a later conflicting update about the same subject.","memory_cards":[{"kind":"current_memory","statement":"mem_new was returned because it is the active memory for Sam from Goldman after conflict resolution.","confidence":"high","entities":[{"name":"Sam","type":"person","role":"subject"},{"name":"Goldman","type":"organization","role":"source"}],"relationships":[{"subject":"mem_new","predicate":"about","object":"Sam"},{"subject":"mem_new","predicate":"sourced_from","object":"Goldman"},{"subject":"mem_new","predicate":"status","object":"current"}]},{"kind":"superseded_memory","statement":"mem_old was filtered because it is superseded and should not be presented as current recall.","confidence":"high","entities":[{"name":"Sam","type":"person","role":"subject"},{"name":"Goldman","type":"organization","role":"source"}],"relationships":[{"subject":"mem_old","predicate":"about","object":"Sam"},{"subject":"mem_old","predicate":"sourced_from","object":"Goldman"},{"subject":"mem_old","predicate":"status","object":"superseded"},{"subject":"mem_new","predicate":"supersedes","object":"mem_old"}]}],"entity_resolution":{"action":"reuse_existing","entity_id":"Sam","reason":"Both memories refer to the same subject, Sam, so recall resolves them to one entity and keeps only the non-superseded version."},"conflict_classification":"supersession","answer":"Recall returned mem_new because it is the latest active memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because it has been explicitly superseded by mem_new, and superseded memories are not shown as current recall results. This preserves the current state while avoiding presentation of outdated or replaced facts.","citations":["mem_new","mem_old"],"repair_options":["Verify that mem_old has a superseded/inactive status flag.","Verify that mem_new is linked to mem_old via a supersedes relation.","If both should remain visible, change retrieval policy to include historical memories separately from current recall.","If supersession was applied incorrectly, remove the supersedes link and reindex both memories."],"receipt":{"success":true,"details":["Resolved Sam as the shared subject across both memories.","Identified mem_new as current and mem_old as superseded.","Applied recall policy to exclude superseded memories from current results."]}}
